content:
# 3D LUT Issue #25 — Closed as Superseded (2026-04-03)

## What: CPU-side 3D LUT for non-keyframe grading was superseded by GPU grading in PR #26

## Why Closed:
Issue #25 targeted the CPU grading LAB roundtrip (~25ms/frame) on non-keyframes. PR #26 wired CudaGrader into flush_buffer_with_depth, so non-keyframes now run through the full GPU pipeline (LUT apply + HSL correct + clarity PTX). The CPU path the LUT was targeting is no longer on the hot path.

At 4K (8.3M pixels), a CPU rayon trilinear lookup would be slower than the GPU path, not faster. The 15-25ms estimate in the issue was for sub-4K resolutions.

## Current bottleneck (v4, post PR #26):
GPU grading at 4K = ~332ms/frame. Total wall time: 8m 41s for 1671-frame test clip.

## Actual next optimization:
Proxy-resolution GPU grading (grade at 1080p, upscale result) → ~4× GPU throughput.
This addresses the real bottleneck, not the LAB roundtrip.

## CUDA 3D texture (future):
A CUDA 3D texture kernel for non-keyframe grading (~5ms/non-keyframe) remains viable but is a different design from the 7 CPU-focused tasks in issue #25. Should be opened as a separate issue.

## No code was implemented (0 of 7 tasks). Closed as not planned.
